Meaning: [Bell., - Abscess or Suppuration THREATENING:Redness, pain and throbbing, without much swelling, Bell 12, 1hrly] [  apis., - Abscess or Suppuration : Much swelling with or without redness, burning, throbbing, stinging pain, Apis 3x, 1hrly] [ merc., - Abscess or Suppuration:After Bell. or Apis, if either of these is insufficient to check the inflammatory action, Merc sol 6, 2hrly] [ tarent., ars., - Abscess or Suppuration:Tissues bluish, intense burning pains, : Tarent cub 6, 2hrly : Ars alb 3, 2hrly] [calc-s., sil., calen., - Abscess or Suppuration :Abscess near the rectum, : Calc sulph gr. vi. 3hrly : Silica 6, 3hrly :]  [hep., - Abscess or Suppuration FORMED :When matter has actually formed, to assist the process and bring it to a favourable termination, Hepar sulph 6, 3hrly]  [ sil., calen., - Abscess or Suppuration FORMED : When suppuration has taken place and is slow to evacuate, Silica 6, 3hrly Locally, fomentations with hot Calendula lotion : two or three times a day. As soon as the bulk of the discharge has come away Calendula ointment should be substituted for the wet dressing] [ sil., calend., - Abscess Chronic or CHRONIC SUPPURATION : Silica 6, 6hrly; locally, locally, a compress of Calendula Q :, to be kept applied, and changed frequently. As soon as the bulk of the discharge has come away Calendula ointment should be substituted for the wet dressing [ gunp., - CHRONIC SUPPURATION Gunpowder. 3x, gr. vi. 6hrly] [ chin., - CHRONIC SUPPURATION With hectic fever; debility from great loss of fluids, China 3, 2hrly.] [ ars., - CHRONIC SUPPURATION With great debility, low fever, red tongue, thirst, restlessness, anxiety, Arsenic alb 12, 2hrly ]  [ fl-ac.,- CHRONIC SUPPURATION = Fistulous openings, Acid fluor 6, gtt. iii. 6hrly ] [ gunp.,arn., pyrog., - ABSCESS OR SUPPURATION : Symptoms of blood-poisoning, : Gunp. 3x, gr. vi. 4hrly : Arn. 3, 4hrly : Pyrogen 30, 4hrly]

Meaning: [ hydr-ac., - Angina Pectoris: PAROXYSM.-Palpitation, anxiety, small pulse; associated with epilepsy, hydr-ac. 3x, th.] [ glon., - Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Violent beating as if heart would burst chest open, laboured breathing, pains radiating in all directions, down left arm with weakness of it, Glon. 3, th.]  [ spig., - Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Pressure and oppression; darting, shooting, stabbing, or lacerating pain, faintness and dyspnœa; pain down left arm; effects of tobacco or alcohol, Spig. 3, th.] [ naja., - Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Nervous irritation depression, pains at the heart, and tremulous irritability of the heart, Naja. 6, th.] [ cact., - Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Pain as if the heart were gripped with an iron hand, constriction of the chest, Cact. 3, 1h.] [ cimic., -Angina Pectoris :PAROXYSM Pain at the heart with rheumatic symptoms, Act racemosa 3, 1h.] [ Cupr., -Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM with asthmatic symptoms and cramps, Cupr. met. 6, th.]  [ Lil-t., -Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Oppression of breathing, with great restlessness; tobacco angina; dragging. down sensation, Lil-t. 30, 8h.] [ Lith., -Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM Pain at heart in morning, on bending forward in bed; or before, and during urination, Lith carb 6, 8h.] [ Aml-n., -Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM If other measures fail to relieve the pain, inhalations of Nitrite of Amyl may be given 3 drops on cotton wool.] [ Naja., -Angina Pectoris:PAROXYSM In the absence of specific indications, Naja 30, a dose every half hour for 2-3 doses, will speedily relieve, if it is going to be useful.] [ Note., - Angina Pectoris: THE DISEASE. Remedies: acon., anac., apis, arn., ars-s-f., aster., bapt., bar-c., bell., brom., bry., cac., cadm-br., cadm-s., carbn-h., carbn-s., caust., chen-a., chin., chlol., coff., crot-h., cupr-acet., fl-ac., form., gast., glon., guare., hell., juni., kali-br., kali-cy., lach., lim., lith-br., nat-ns., nux-m., nux-v., oena., op., ran-g., sep., sin-n., sol-a., stram., stront-c., tab., verat., verat-v.,note.,
Meaning: [ Nux-v., - Apoplexy, THREATENED : Giddiness, headache, fullness in the head in plethoric subjects, Nux v 3, 3h.avoidance of all stimulating drink or food, and all excitement.] [ Acon., Apoplexy Threatened : Numbness and tingling, with arterial excitement, Acon. 3, 2h.] [ aster., - Apoplexy Threatened :Rush of blood to head, Asteria rubens. 12, 1h.] [ Bell., -Apoplexy EARLY SYMPTOMS: Full, throbbing head with flushed face, Bell. 3, 2h. Hot fomentations to the head.]  [Acon., -Apoplexy FIT OF : Quick, full pulse, Acon. 12, every quarter of an hour.] [Bell., -Apoplexy FIT OF : Great redness of the face, and signs of active congestion of the head, Bell. 12, every quarter of an hour.] [Nux-v., -Apoplexy FIT OF : In cases of less active congestion and fever, where there have been errors of diet, Nux v. 3, every quarter of an hour.] [Op., -Apoplexy FIT OF : Dusky-red face, coma stertor, Opium 3, every quarter of an hour.] [Arn., -Apoplexy FIT OF : Absence of signs of active congestion. Arn. 3, every quarter of an hour.] [Ferr-p., -Apoplexy FIT OF : Ferr. phos. 6x, in water, a teaspoonful every hour until sensibility is recovered.] [Arn.,Kali-m., -Apoplexy AFTER-EFFECTS : When sensibility is recovered, Arn. 3, 1h. & Kali-m. 6x, four times daily, is a very useful remedy, and may be used in alternation with Arn. 3. It is advisable to continue its use for several weeks.] [Note., -Apoplexy Resulting paralysis: see Paralysis.]

Rubrics: Homeosetu Clinical Repertory; Clarke; Asthma :
Remedies: acetan., acon., am-c., ambr., ammc., amyg-am., ant-i., ant-t., apis, aral., arg-cy., ars., arum-d., arum-m., asaf., asc-t., aur., aur-m., bar-m., benz-ac., blatta, blatta-a., brom., bry., cac., calad., calc-ar., calc-hp., cann-s., caps., carb-v., chen-a., chin., chin-ar., chin-s., chlol., chlor., cina, coc-c., coca, coch., coff., colch., con., cupr., der., dig., digin., dros., erio., eucal., eupi., fel., ferr., gad., gall-ac., galv., grin., hep., hydr., hydr-ac., hyper., iber., ictod., ip., junc., kali-bi., kali-br., kali-c., kali-chl., kali-chls., kali-cy., kali-n., kali-p., kali-s., lac-d., lach., lact., laur., led., lem-m., linu-u., lob., lob-s., lyc., magn-gl., manc., mand., med., meph., merc-i-r., mill., naja, nat-s., nuph., nux-v., ol-an., ol-j., osm., pect., ph-ac., phel., phos., phos-pchl., phyt., plb., pop-c., psor., ptel., pulm-v., queb., rumx., samb., samb-c., sang., sanguin-n., sanic., sars., seneg., silphu., spong., squil., stann., stict., stry., succ., sul-h., sulph., sumb., syph., tab., ter., thuj., trach., trio., tub., vario., verat., verat-v., wye., xan., zinc., zinc-val., zing., ziz.
Meaning:
[ Hydr-ac., - Asthma PAROXYSM : Recent and uncomplicated, Hydrocy. ac. 3x, 15m.]  [ Cupr., - Asthma : Pure spasmodic asthma, spasm very prominent all over body, vomiting after the attack, Cupr met. 6, 15m.]  Attack occurring early in morning: frequently induced by disorders of stomach, Nux vom. 3, 15m. In hydrogenoid subjects; with morning diarrhea, Nat. sul. 3, gr. iv. 4h Occurring periodically at midnight or 2 a.m., Ars. 3, 15m, Occurring from 3 to 5 a.m., Kali c. 6, 15m. Pulse full, anxiousness, restlessness, fear, Acon. 3, 15m. Useful in many cases, especially in stout people and when there is a malarial element, Blatt. 2x-3x, 2h. in the attack, 6-30, 6h. in interval. Convulsive breathing, nausea or vomiting, cold sweat on the face, Verat. v. 3, 15m. Nausea and great depression of heart, Lobel. 3, gtt. i. 15m. Old asthmatics, Senega 1, 4h. Where the asthma is not pure, but associated with catarrh and cough, if the mucus is scanty, Ipec. 3, 15m, If the mucus is profuse, Ant. t. 6, 15m. When there is a history of former attacks of bronchitis and pneumonia, Ars, i. 3x, gr. ii. or gtt. ii. after meals. If these fail, Stramonium cigarettes may be smoked as a palliative. If possible, how- ever, this should be avoided as the use of them is a distinct hindrance to the cure of the condition.  INTERVALS. Spasmodic variety; vomiting after attack, Cupr. m. 6, 6h. Attacks occurring early in morning, stomach disorders, Nux v. 3, 6h. Where there is, in addition to the attacks, general debility, loss of flesh, tendency to night sweats, bowels loose or regular, tongue clean or red, relief by heat, attacks worse in cold, damp weather, Ars. 3, 6h. Chronic asthma; sudden suppression of chronic eruptions; psoriasis; gout; tendency to skin eruptions; patient subject to fainting spells; sinking sensation in the forenoon; flushes of heat, Sulph. 3, 6h. Malarial or sycotic cases; in damp localities, near lakes or rivers; < early morning, Nat. sul. 3, gr. viii. 4h. : In most cases a course of Bacil. 30-200 once a week will be of marked advantage.
[ Cupr., - Asthma INTERVALS: Spasmodic variety; vomiting after attack, Cupr m 6, 6h.] [

Meaning: [ Note., - Biliousness : Patients who are subject to periodical attacks of biliousness should take regular exercise, avoid alcoholic drinks of all kinds, eat sparingly of meat, and avoid rich or fat food. The removal of the tendency may be assisted by the following medicines, which must be given according to the symptoms of the patient over a length of time] [ Ferr., - Biliousness : In bloodless subjects, with pale or flushed face, and throbbing head, Ferr. 6, 4h.]  [ kali-c., - Biliousness :In persons of costive habit, subject to one-sided headache, Kali-c. 6, 8h.] [ nux-v., - Biliousness :  In dark, sallow, spare persons of sedentary life, subject to constipation, Nux v. 3, 8h.]  [ sulph., - Biliousness : Persons who suffer from acidity, sinking at pit of stomach in forenoon, fainty spells, constipation and piles, Sulph. 6, 8h.]  [ nat-s., - Biliousness : In gouty subjects, with loaded urine, Nat-s., 6x, gr. v. 8h.]  [ lyc., - Biliousness : When there is abdominal flatulence, constipation, and scanty urine, Lyc. 6, 8h.]  [ mag-m.,chel., chion., - Biliousness : Discomfort in the region of the liver, constipation, the stools being composed of very minute lumps, packed together like sheeps', Mag-m. 6, 8h. Chel. Q 5-10 drops in a little water 3 times daily after meals is an extremely valuable adjunct to treatment. Chionanthus & similarly, either of these two remedies can be taken in addition to other treatment.] [ Note., - Biliousness : See also Anæmia, Liver, Constipation, Diarrhœa, etc.]

Meaning: [ Bry.,Phyt., Bry., Bell.,hep.,calend.,sil., - Breast, ABSCESS OF ,Threatening: as soon as the first symptoms of pain or hardness come on, Bry. 3, 1h. This will often abort the process. Less hardness than Bry. red streaks radiating from a central point to the circumference, Bell. 3, 1h. Should suppuration occur, give Hep. 6, 3h. Meaning: [ Acon., -Bronchitis:In the earliest stage, chills, fever, oppression, dry, tickling cough, Acon. 3, 1h.] [ Bry., -Bronchitis:Fever established, dry, hacking cough, or with a little mucus, hoarseness, soreness along trachea; pains between shoulders, sharp pains in chest; tongue white, constipation, Bry. 3, 1h.]  [ Bell., -Bronchitis:Short dry cough from tickling in larynx; dry spasmodic cough with vomiting; stitches in chest; headache, redness and heat of face; bronchitis after ether anæsthesia, Bell. 1-30, 1h.] [ Merc., -Bronchitis:Cough looser, tendency to perspiration, Merc. 6, 3h.]  [ Ip., -Bronchitis :Cough spasmodic, great dyspnea, little expectoration, dry sounds in chest in day, moist at night, symptoms worst at night; with gastrointestinal disturbance and persistent nausea, Ip. 3, 1h.] [ Ant-t., -Bronchitis:Oppression of chest, great depression, cough and hoarseness, much expectoration, clear or white; tongue white, creamy, disgust for food, inclination to vomit; capillary bronchitis in children, Ant-t. 6, 2h.] [ Kali-bi., -Bronchitis:Acute or chronic bronchitis, where the mucus is tough and stringy and difficult to raise, Kali-bi. 3x, 2h.]  [ Ars., -Bronchitis:Cough spasmodic, waking the patient up in the night, very little expectoration, Ars. 3, 2h.] [ Ars-i., -Bronchitis:Bronchitis with fever and night sweat, purulent expectoration; phthisical symptoms; great weakness, Ars-i. 3x, gr. iii. thrice daily immediately after food.]  [ Phos., -Bronchitis:Broncho pneumonia, Phos. 3, 2h.]  [ Sang., -Bronchitis:Spasmodic cough, dry or with copious expectoration, oppression of the chest, with stitches or burning; often accompanied with nasal catarrh, Sang. 3, 1h.] [ hep., -Bronchitis:Somewhat chronic bronchitis, profuse yellow expectoration, hoarseness, sensation of a clot of mucus, or internal swelling when swallowing, Hep. 6, 2h.]  [ Puls., -Bronchitis:In delicate blonde subjects, lax fibre, abundant expectoration; cough < n lying down; compels the patient to sit up in bed; <in warm room, Puls. 3, 2h.] [ sulph., -Bronchitis:Chronic cases, after subsidence of acute symptoms, tendency to headache, liver sluggish, asthmatic, constipation; < when warm in bed at night, Sulph. 3, 4h.]  [ nit-ac., -Bronchitis:Acute or chronic cases, much oppression at chest; cough worst night and morning; dry except after night's rest; short or spasmodic general health affected, Nit-ac. 6, 2h.] [ Carb-v., -Bronchitis:In old persons with blue nails and cold extremities, hoarse, profuse expectoration without power to raise it, Carb-v. 6, 2h.] [ Am-c., -Bronchitis: Long-standing bronchitis in old people, physical powers depressed, expectoration difficult to raise from want of power, Am-c. 3x, 1h.] [ Seneg., -Bronchitis:In old people, cough irritating and shaking, Seneg. 3, 2h.] [ Caust., -Bronchitis:Where there is irritability of the bladder, the cough causing expulsion of urine, Caust. 6, 3h.] [ Rumx., -Bronchitis:Spasmodic cough with difficult expulsion of phlegm, and feeling of soreness all down trachea and under sternum; < in cold air, Rumex. 6, 2h.] [ Hyos., -Bronchitis:Cough coming on on lying down, Hyos. 3, 2h.] [ Con., -Bronchitis: Short, dry cough from tickle under middle of sternum, coming on when lying down at night, Con. 3, 2h. : may be given at night, whilst others are given by day, should the general symptoms indicate other medicines, and the night cough call for one of these.)] [ Tub-av., -Bronchitis:Bronchitis of Influenza, suspiciously like Tuberculosis Aviare 30, 4h.] [ Kali-i., -Bronchitis: After effects, Kali iod. 30, 4h.]  [ Note., -Bronchitis:See also Cough.]

Q, gtt. v. 4h.]  [ Bufo., Bapt., - OPEN CANCERS ACTUAL ULCERATION : Foetid discharge : Bufo. 30, 4h. : Bapt. 1x, 3h.; a lotion of Bapt. Q : to be injected into the wound every few hours.] [ Hydr.,gali., - OPEN CANCERS ACTUAL ULCERATION : If the ulceration is angry, irritable, and increasing, Hydr. 1x, 3h. being given internally, Hydr. ointment : to be painted on with a camel-hair brush, after syringing with a lotion of Hydr. Q ten drops, strong carbolic acid five drops, water one ounce. Should this fail to relieve, Gali. Q, 6h.; locally, an application of equal parts of Gali. Q and glycerine.] [ Hydr.,ham.,ruta., - OPEN CANCERS ACTUAL ULCERATION : Where there is bleeding, Sang. 1x, 2h.; locally a lotion of Ham.Q :. A useful ointment in open cancers is Ruta. ointment, made by extracting the whole fresh plant in vaseline. The ordinary Boracic acid ointment is also useful in many cases.]

Rubrics: Homeosetu Clinical Repertory; Clarke; Cholera; Asiatica :
Meaning: [ Note., - Cholera Asiatica: PREVENTION.-Wear next the skin a plate of copper :. Let it be fastened round the waist by straps attached to longitudinal slits cut in the ends of the plate, which should be oval. Let the plate rest on the front of the abdominal wall and let it be made slightly concave, so as to adapt itself to the shape of the body. The plate should be worn day and night. It may be cleansed from time to time by rubbing with vinegar.]  [ Camph., -Cholera Asiatica: When Cholera is epidemic, Tincture of Camphor should be taken once or twice a day, in doses of two or three drops on sugar. Can cause nausea if taken in water.]   [ Camph., -Cholera Asiatica: PRELIMINARY SYMPTOMS. In cholera times, whenever diarrhœa occurs, give Rubini's Tincture of Camphor, three drops on a lump of sugar, every fifteen minutes to every hour, according to the urgency of the symptoms, until the diarrhœa is completely removed.Should an attack come on without premonitory diarrhœa, sudden coldness and lividity seizing the patient, give Rubini's Camphor, three drops every ten minutes. This may be given if there is diarrhœa at the same time, provided it is not excessive.] [ Verat., -Cholera Asiatica:If under Camphor reaction does not come on, but, on the contrary, the patient becomes worse, vomiting and purging setting in with violent pains in the body, cold sweat on forehead, give Verat. 1, gtt. ii. 10m.]  [ Cupr-a., -Cholera Asiatica:If the patient complains much of cramps with the vomiting and purging, Cupr-a. 3x. gtt. ii. 10m.]  [ Ars., -Cholera Asiatica:If the collapse deepens in spite of treatment, and the patient is in danger more from general depression than the discharges, Ars. 3x. gtt. i. every ten minutes.]   [  Hydr-ac., -Cholera Asiatica:When the collapse is most profound, respiration slow, deep, gasping, the patient appearing dead in the intervals; collapse supervening on cessation of discharge, Hydr-ac. 1, gtt. ii. 10m.]  [ Ric., -Cholera Asiatica:Rice- water stools, painless, Ric., 1, gtt. ii. 1h.]  [ Colch., -Cholera Asiatica:With tympanites, Colch. 3x. gtt. ii. 1/2—1h.]

Meaning: [ Nux-v.,Sulph.,-Constipation. In sedentary people, dark, spare; ineffectual urging; frequent desire, but only very little passes, Nux-v. 1-30, 8h. After Nux, if this is insufficient; in persons who are subject to skin eruptions; who suffer from fainting spells, flushing of heat to the head, or sinking sensation at the pit of the stomach, especially about 11 a.m.; frequent ineffectual urging to stool, insufficient stool, sensation as if something remained behind in rectum, piles which bleed periodically, Sulph. 3-30, 8h.] [ Bry.,-Torpor of bowels, stool hard, large, dry, Bry. 3, 6h.]  [ Op.,-Torpor of bowels; stool small, hard pieces like marbles; dark brown, with drowsiness, Op. 3, 6h.]  [ Nux-m.,-Drowsiness, chilliness, flatulence, Nux-m. 30, 6h.] [ plb-acet.,-Very obstinate constipation, dry, lumpy stool; painless, or with severe colic and retraction of abdomen, Plb-acet. 3, gr. ii. -6, 6h.]  [ Nit-ac.,-Hard, scanty stool, painful in passing, burning in rectum, passage of blood, Nit-ac. 1, 4h.]  [ Graph.,-Large, knotty stool, covered with white shreds of mucus, expelled with much effort; associated with delayed menses. Graph. 6, 6h.] [ Aesc.,- Hard knotty stool, with or without blind piles, much pain in the back; sensation of fullness in rectum after stool; sensation in rectum as if full of small sticks, Aesc. 1, 6h.]  [ Chin.,-Accumulation of faeces in rectum, Chin. 1, 2h.]  [ Mag-m.,-Stool like sheep's dung; pain in region of liver, Mag-m. 5, 6h.] [ Caust., -Stool tough, shiny, knotty, like sheep's dung, oily; pressure ir rectum as if fæces lodged in it, Caust. 5, gtt. ii. 4h.]  [ Zinc.,-Stool hard, small, dry, crumbling, Zinc., 6, 4h.]  [ Zinc-m., - Black, pitchy stool, Zinc-m. 3, 4h.]  [ Nat-m.,-Stool retained, sensation as if rough fæces remained in rectum, feeling of constriction at anus arresting it; especially in illnourished persons with unhealthy complexion; associated with deficient menses, Nat-m. 3, gr. ii. 6, 6h.] [ Sep.,-Slow, insufficient stool; sensation of weight or ball in anus not relieved by stool, Sep. 6, 4h.]  [ Lyc.,-With distention of the abdomen, flatulence passing down- wards, water high-coloured, with deposit of lithates, hard, difficult stool, Lyc. 6, 6h.]  [ Alum.,-Dilated and paralysed rectum; lumpy stools, Alum., 6, 6h.]  [ Alumn.,-Stools hard as stones; passes much blood; constipation of uterine or rectal cancer, Alumn., 30, 6h.] [ Hydr.,-No desire for stool; constipation alternating with looseness of the bowels; constipation with dull headache; after abuse of purgatives; with foul tongue; with piles, Hydr. 1, 6h.]  [ Verat.,-Stool difficult to pass on account of hardness and size, Verat. 3, 6h.] [ Merc-d.,Sulph.,-Constipation, Palliatives: A satisfactory evacuation can often be obtained by Merc-d. ix. gr. iii. 4h. for a few doses. Persons who have been in the habit of taking purga tives and fear to leave them off, Sulph. $, at bedtime. A glass of cold water drunk fasting will often suffice to ensure a good evacuation. Or a glass of cold water may be taken at bedtime; or, if cold water is not tolerated, hot water instead. Or this: a tablespoonful of coarse treacle put into a tumbler of water overnight, and drunk by sips in the morning whilst dressing. In constipation in infants, manna used for sweetening their food is often of great service.] [ Note., - See under DIET-INFANTS.]  [ Hydr.,- Constipation Palliative ,If other things fail, Hydr $, gtt. iii. in a wine-glassful of water, taken in the morning fasting, acts 351 mild aperient. A sitz-bath every second night :, for five or ten minutes, the body and limbs being kept thoroughly warm during the time, is often of great assistance where there is torpor of the bowels. A cold water or tepid water compress may be worn across the body at night.] [ Note.Spig.,Gels., Iris.,- Whenever constipation is one of many symptoms of disordered health, the medicines directed to the chief disorder will usually remove the constipation also: Spigelia in heart affections, Iris. in migraine, Gels. in headaches.]
